subject: Re: phase plate data
body:
Hi Dimitry,

I have the same issues with Giulia.

We recently collected the dataset with Volta phase plate using Krios and I 
found a couple of issues.

(1) the raw image looks good but have a bad thon ring. Not surprisingly, 
CTF failed.
(2) CTF gets reasonable value around 3-5A (defocus range 0-2um), but only 
1/2 or 1/3 of the total particles seem to be picked.
     I am using BoxNet2Mask_20180918 for picking particles.

At least, picked particles look good though.

Do you know what is wrong?

Best, Uhnsoo Cho


On Thursday, September 27, 2018 at 8:50:16 AM UTC-4, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> Hi Giulia,
>
> I haven't encountered issues with fitting phase plate data so far. Would 
> it be possible to share one of the problematic micrographs, or an image of 
> the CTF fit? 
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
>
> On Monday, September 24, 2018 at 4:50:13 PM UTC+2, Giulia Z wrote:
>>
>> Hi, I'm trying warp on a phase plate dataset, but ctf isn't making sense. 
>> I can see the rings, and I have tried to play with all parameters, with 
>> no luck.
>>
>> I am not sure how to troubleshoot, maybe I'm making some obvious mistake.
>>
>> This is krios/K2 data, energy filter at 20eV, pixel size 0.85A, dose is 
>> 1.3 e/A/frame, defocus range 0-3um.
>>
>> If anybody has suggestions for parameters to fit this collection 
>> condition I'd be happy to hear!
>>
>> Thanks
>> Giulia
>>
>> p.s. it works just fine on no-phase plate data
>>
>
